entries.desc: This report shows that Knowledge Is Power Program (KIPP) middle schools have significant and substantial positive impacts on student achievement in four core academic subjects: reading, math, science, and social studies.

entries.title: The Teacher-Student Data Link Project: First-Year Implementation
entries.desc: The Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation contracted with Mathematica to conduct an implementation study of the Teacher-Student Data Link project, which aims to support states in developing best practices for linking student and teacher data to improve educational outcomes.

entries.title: Effectiveness of Four Supplemental Reading Comprehension Interventions
entries.desc: This article presents evidence from a large-scale randomized controlled trial of the effects of four supplemental reading comprehension curricula (Project CRISS, ReadAbout, Read for Real, and Reading for Knowledge).

entries.title: Measurement Models for Behavioral Frequencies: A Comparison Between Numerically and Vaguely Quantified Reports
entries.desc: Surveys collecting behavioral frequency information request either numerically or vaguely quantified frequency reports.

entries.title: Impacts of Title I Supplemental Educational Services on Student Achievement
entries.desc: As part of No Child Left Behind, parents of low-income students in low-performing schools are offered Supplemental Educational Services (SES) for their children. These academic supports, such as extra tutoring or group sessions, take place outside the regular school day.

entries.title: Replicating Experimental Impact Estimates Using a Regression Discontinuity Approach
entries.desc: This report attempts to replicate the estimated impacts from experimental studies of two different education interventions using a regression discontinuity design.

entries.title: Using an Experimental Evaluation of Charter Schools to Test Whether Nonexperimental Comparison Group Methods Can Replicate Experimental Impact Estimates
entries.desc: Using data from Mathematica's experimental evaluation of charter schools, this methodological study examines the validity of four different comparison group approaches to test whether these designs can replicate findings from a well-implemented random assignment study.

entries.title: Value-Added Estimates for Phase 1 of the Pennsylvania Teacher and Principal Evaluation Pilot
entries.desc: Mathematica provided technical assistance in developing and estimating VAMs for use in Phase 1 of the pilot.

entries.title: Learning from Charter School Management Organizations: Strategies for Student Behavior and Teacher Coaching
entries.desc: A new Mathematica study, conducted with the Center on Reinventing Public Education, highlights approaches five successful charter school management organizations (CMOs) use to help improve student achievement.

entries.title: An Evaluation of the Chicago Teacher Advancement Program (Chicago TAP) After Four Years
entries.desc: Mathematica's final report on the Chicago Teacher Advancement Program (Chicago TAP) found that the program did not raise student math or reading scores, but it increased teacher retention in some schools.

entries.title: Charter-School Management Organizations: Diverse Strategies and Diverse Student Impacts
entries.desc: A new analysis from the National Study of Charter Management (CMO) Effectiveness provides the first systematic evidence available on the effects of CMOs on the critical long-term outcomes of high school graduation and college entry.

entries.title: High School Dual Enrollment Programs: Are We Fast-Tracking Students Too Fast?
entries.desc: This study tracked a subset of Florida's 2000–2001 and 2001–2002 high school seniors who took a college algebra placement test.

entries.title: Determinants of Students' Success: The Role of Advanced Placement and Dual Enrollment Programs
entries.desc: This study tracked all of Florida's 2000–2001 and 2001–2002 high school seniors and found that students who participated in dual enrollment classes on college campuses were 12 percent more likely to go to college and 7 percent more likely to earn a bachelor's degree than similar students who did not participate.

entries.title: Impacts of Performance Pay Under the Teacher Incentive Fund: Study Design Report
entries.desc: Mathematica's rigorous evaluation of the Teacher Incentive Fund (TIF) will estimate the impact of performance pay on student achievement, as well as educators' attitudes toward and awareness of TIF and their mobility and recruitment.

entries.title: Variability in Pretest-Posttest Correlation Coefficients by Student Achievement Level
entries.desc: State assessments are increasingly used as outcome measures for education evaluations, and such evaluations generally use pretest scores as control variables.

entries.title: Do Typical RCTs of Education Interventions Have Sufficient Statistical Power for Linking Impacts on Teacher Practice and Student Achievement Outcomes? (Journal Article)
entries.desc: For randomized controlled trials (RCTs) of education interventions, estimates of associations between student and mediating teacher practice outcomes can help examine the extent to which the data support the study's conceptual model.

entries.title: Estimation and Identification of the Complier Average Causal Effect Parameter in Education RCTs
entries.desc: In randomized control trials (RCTs) in the education field, the complier average causal effect (CACE) parameter is often of policy interest, because it pertains to intervention effects for students who receive a meaningful dose of treatment services.

entries.title: Evaluating ARRA Programs and Other Educational Reforms: A Guide for States
entries.desc: The American Institutes for Research and Mathematica developed this guide to help you consider evaluation issues likely to arise as you launch ARRA-funded initiatives and other educational reform activities.

entries.title: The Effects of Charter High Schools on Educational Attainment
entries.desc: This article analyzes the relationship between charter high school attendance and educational attainment in Florida and Chicago.

entries.title: Funding Special Education by Capitation: Evidence from State Finance Reforms
entries.desc: This study examined responses to state capitation policies for special education finance between 1991 to 1992 and 2003 to 2004. Capitation refers to distributing funds based on the entire student enrollment.

entries.title: Estimating the Return to College Selectivity Over the Career Using Administrative Earning Data
entries.desc: This paper uses administrative earnings data to the effect of attending a highly selective college on future earnings. It extends the work of a previous paper—that examined the 1995 earnings of a cohort of students who entered college in 1976.

entries.title: Teacher Career Paths, Teacher Quality, and Persistence in the Classroom: Are Public Schools Keeping Their Best?
entries.desc: This paper examines the mobility of early-career teachers of varying quality—measured using value-added methods—and focuses on the variation in these effects across the effectiveness distribution. On average, more effective teachers remain in the profession and stay in their initial schools.

entries.title: The Impacts of Philadelphia's Accelerated Schools on Academic Progress and Graduation
entries.desc: This report evaluates the impacts of Philadelphia’s accelerated schools—alternative high schools serving students at high risk of dropping out—on enrollees’ graduation rates and on rates of credit accumulation for recent enrollees.

entries.title: Estimating Teacher and School Effectiveness in Pittsburgh: Value-Added Modeling and Results
entries.desc: This report describes Mathematica's work in developing value-added models (VAMs) that estimate teachers’ and schools’ contributions to the achievement of their students, and assisting the Pittsburgh Public Schools in developing performance-based pay plans for teachers and principals.

entries.title: Disabled or Young? Relative Age and Special Education Diagnoses in Schools
entries.desc: This study extends recent findings of a relationship between the relative age of students among their peers and their probability of disability classification.

entries.title: Achievement Effects of Four Early Elementary School Math Curricula: Findings for First and Second Graders
entries.desc: This report, the second from a large-scale study for the U.S. Department of Education’s Institute of Education Sciences of four early math programs, shows that the curriculum used by the study schools mattered.

entries.title: Achievement Effects of Four Early Elementary School Math Curricula: Findings for First and Second Graders (Issue Brief)
entries.desc: This study examines whether some early elementary school math curricula are more effective than others at improving math achievement in schools serving a high percentage of disadvantaged students.

entries.title: Efficacy of Schoolwide Programs to Promote Social and Character Development and Reduce Problem Behavior in Elementary School Children
entries.desc: Provides results from an evaluation of seven social and character development programs.

entries.title: The Late Pretest Problem in Randomized Control Trials of Education Interventions (Journal Article)
entries.desc: To improve precision of estimated treatment effects, education randomized control trials (RCTs) often use pretest–post-test experimental designs.

entries.title: Measuring School and Teacher Value Added for IMPACT and TEAM in DC Public Schools
entries.desc: This report describes the design of a value-added model to measure school and teacher performance in the District of Columbia Public Schools. It presents the main features of the method in nontechnical terms, data used, and technical details of the statistical methods.

entries.title: An Experimental Evaluation of Four Elementary School Math Curricula
entries.desc: This article examines the effectiveness of four distinct elementary school math curricula: Investigations in Number, Data, and Space®; Math Expressions; Saxon Math™; and Scott Foresman-Addison Wesley Mathematics (SFAW).

entries.title: Does the Amount of Participation in Afterschool Programs Relate to Developmental Outcomes? A Review of the Literature
entries.desc: Contrary to the findings from previous reviews we found little support for the general notion that greater amounts of participation in afterschool programs was related to academic, behavioral, or socio-emotional outcomes.

entries.title: Standards for Regression Discontinuity Designs
entries.desc: Regression discontinuity (RD) designs are increasingly used by researchers to obtain unbiased estimates of the effects of education-related interventions.

entries.title: The Unknown World of Charter High Schools
entries.desc: In this study we use data from Chicago and Florida to estimate the effects of attending a charter high school on the likelihood that a student will complete high school and attend college.

entries.title: Effectiveness of Selected Supplemental Reading Comprehension Interventions: Findings from Two Student Cohorts
entries.desc: This study evaluated the effectiveness of four supplemental reading comprehension programs in helping disadvantaged fifth graders improve reading comprehension.

entries.title: An Evaluation of the Teacher Advancement Program (TAP) in Chicago: Year Two Impact Report
entries.desc: This report details second-year results from Mathematica’s study on Chicago Public Schools’ Teacher Advancement Program (TAP), a Teacher Incentive Fund grantee.

entries.title: Student Characteristics and Outcomes in Alternative and Neighborhood High Schools in Philadelphia
entries.desc: This report presents descriptive analyses of the enrollment patterns, background characteristics, test scores, and graduation rates of students enrolled in three types of public high schools in Philadelphia: alternative disciplinary schools; alternative accelerated schools for students who struggled academically in regular public high schools; and regular, neighborhood-zoned high schools.

entries.title: Roads to Success: Estimated Impacts of an Education and Career Planning Program During Middle School
entries.desc: This report assesses the impact of two years of exposure to the school and career planning program, Roads to Success, on eighth graders from high-poverty rural schools. The main findings are that the program had no measurable effect on desire to learn job skills or study/learning habits.

entries.title: Compendium of Student, Teacher, and Classroom Measures Used in NCEE Evaluations of Educational Interventions, Volume I: Measures Selection Approaches and Compendium Development Methods
entries.desc: This report contains resources to help researchers and policymakers review measures used in NCEE evaluations of educational interventions.

entries.title: Compendium of Student, Teacher, and Classroom Measures Used in NCEE Evaluations of Educational Interventions, Volume II: Technical Details, Measure Profiles, and Glossary
entries.desc: This report contains resources to help researchers and policymakers review measures used in NCEE evaluations of educational interventions.

entries.title: College Experiences Survey: Methodological Summary
entries.desc: This report summarizes the College Experiences Survey data collection procedures and results.

entries.title: A Simulation Study to Compare Weighting Methods for Survey Nonresponses in the National Survey of Recent College Graduates
entries.desc: Common methods to adjust sampling weights to account for survey nonresponse are the weighting cell technique, response propensity modeling, or a combination of both.

entries.title: Survey of Outcomes Measurement in Research on Character Education Programs
entries.desc: Through the Partnerships in Character Education Program, the federal government distributes about $25 million annually to state and local education agencies for character education programs. This report documents the constructs measured in studies of 36 randomly selected programs.

entries.title: Training Ph.D.s: The Views of Faculty on Their Role and Their Institutions' Role in Promoting the Development of Responsible Researchers
entries.desc: The development of skilled and ethical researchers in the United States is a well documented national objective. A core component of this objective is to train and educate new researchers about ethical research standards.

entries.title: Do Typical RCTs of Education Interventions Have Sufficient Statistical Power for Linking Impacts on Teacher Practice and Student Achievement Outcomes?
entries.desc: For randomized control trials (RCTs) of education interventions, it is often of interest to estimate associations between student and mediating teacher practice outcomes, to examine the extent to which a study’s conceptual model is supported by data, and to identify mediators most associated with student learning. This paper develops statistical power formulas for such exploratory analyses under clustered school-based RCTs using ordinary least squares (OLS) and instrumental variable (IV) estimat

entries.title: ABCTE Teachers in Florida and Their Effect on Student Performance
entries.desc: Do alternatively certified teachers produce student achievement gains on par with other teachers? This study used propensity score matching to identify a comparison group of teachers in Florida to address this question for the American Board for Certification of Teacher Excellence (ABCTE).

entries.title: Technical Methods Report: The Estimation of Average Treatment Effects for Clustered RCTs of Education Interventions
entries.desc: This paper examines the estimation of two-stage clustered randomized controlled trial designs (RCTs) in education research using the Neyman causal inference framework that underlies experiments.

entries.title: Evaluation of Experience Corps: Student Reading Outcomes
entries.desc: A Mathematica team led by Emily Dwoyer, Kathy Sonnenfeld, and Susan Sprachman provided the data collection services for this study of the Experience Corps (EC) initiative, a program that brings older adults ages 55 and up into public elementary schools to tutor and mentor children who are at risk of academic failure. EC members also help teachers in the classroom and lead after-school enrichment activities.

entries.title: Comprehensive Teacher Induction: Year 1 Impact Findings from an RCT
entries.desc: Comprehensive teacher induction provides novice teachers with carefully selected and trained full-time mentors, as well as a curriculum of intensive and structured support.

entries.title: Effectiveness of Selected Supplemental Reading Comprehension Interventions: Impacts on a First Cohort of Fifth-Grade Students
entries.desc: To become successful learners, students need to comprehend what they read. Children from disadvantaged backgrounds, in particular, may have difficulty comprehending text because they often lack general vocabulary and strategies for organizing information and gleaning knowledge from text.

entries.title: Does the Missouri Teacher Career Ladder Program Raise Student Achievement?
entries.desc: This paper examines the program's effect on student achievement across the state, using longitudinal data on district math and reading scores for 524 Missouri school districts over a nine-year period.

entries.title: Teacher Bonuses for Extra Work: A Profile of Missouri's Career Ladder Program
entries.desc: Based on official program documentation, focus groups with teachers, and telephone interviews with school officials, this report compares how the program works in practice with how it works in theory.

entries.title: Effects of the Missouri Career Ladder Program on Teacher Mobility
entries.desc: This report presents evidence suggesting that a school district’s participation in the Missouri Career Ladder Program would tend to increase retention in the district and the profession, especially for mid-career teachers.

entries.title: Kauffman Firm Survey: Baseline/First/Second/Third Follow-Ups Study Metadata Documentation
entries.desc: Mathematica is conducting a study of new businesses to help the Foundation in its efforts to promote new business development.

entries.title: An Evaluation of the Teacher Advancement Program (TAP) in Chicago: Year One Impact Report
entries.desc: The Teacher Advancement Program (TAP), a whole-school intervention that aims to improve schools by raising teacher quality, provides teachers with opportunities for professional growth, promotion to school leadership roles without leaving the classroom, structured feedback, and performance-based compensation. This report focuses on the Chicago Public Schools, which began implementing TAP in 2007. Early findings from Mathematica’s study, which focused on the district’s K-8 schools, note that teac

entries.title: Baseline Statistics for Evaluation of the Effective Practice Incentive Community
entries.desc: This report provides a general overview of EPIC, compares student characteristics across the three partners, and compares the data used to determine which schools receive EPIC awards with data on all the students attending these schools.

entries.title: An Evaluation of Teachers Trained Through Different Routes to Certification
entries.desc: Mathematica’s random assignment study of alternative routes to teacher certification tracked 2,600 students in 63 schools in 20 medium and large school districts in 7 states during the 2004-2005 and 2005-2006 school years; focused on less selective alternative certification programs producing large numbers of teachers in a range of school districts, from rural to urban; and collected detailed data on coursework requirements and content from 80 teacher training programs. Researchers found no stat

entries.title: Both Sides Now: The Story of School Desegregations Graduates
entries.desc: This book, based on a five-year study of six high schools that had undergone some form of desegregation in the late 1970s, provides a firsthand account of how desegregation affected students during high school and later in life.

entries.title: The Impacts of Regular Upward Bound on Postsecondary Outcomes 7-9 Years After Scheduled High School Graduation
entries.desc: This last report from Mathematica’s evaluation of Upward Bound analyzes data from the final round of survey and transcript data collection as well as administrative records from the National Student Clearinghouse and the federal Student Financial Aid files.

entries.title: Experience Corps: Effects on Student Reading
entries.desc: The Experience Corps (EC) program brings adults aged 55+ into public elementary schools to work with children who are at risk of academic failure.

entries.title: Mission and Money: Understanding the University
entries.desc: Many academic institutions pursue mission-related activities that are unprofitable and engage in profitable revenue raising activities to finance them. This book presents research on schools’ revenue sources from tuition, donations, research, patents, endowments, and other activities.

entries.title: The Late Pretest Problem in Randomized Control Trials of Education Interventions
entries.desc: Pretest and post-test experimental designs are often used in randomized control trials (RCTs) in the education field to improve the precision of the estimated treatment effects.

entries.title: Impacts of Comprehensive Teacher Induction: Results from the First Year of a Randomized Controlled Study
entries.desc: Comprehensive teacher induction provides novice teachers with carefully selected and trained full-time mentors, as well as a curriculum of intensive and structured support.

entries.title: Going Beyond Test Scores: Evaluating Charter School Impact on Educational Attainment in Chicago and Florida
entries.desc: Unlike past charter school studies focused on student achievement, the authors analyzed the relationship between attendance and educational attainment in charter high schools.

entries.title: Bring Them Back, Move Them Forward: Case Studies of Programs Preparing Out-of-School Youths for Further Education and Careers
entries.desc: This report summarizes the results of case studies of six programs designed to help out of school youth earn a high school diploma or GED and prepare for postsecondary education and jobs.

entries.title: Passport to Teaching: Career Choices and Experiences of American Board Certified Teachers
entries.desc: In this report, we examine the career trajectories of those who have successfully completed the American Board Passport to Teaching certification.

entries.title: Guidelines for Multiple Testing in Impact Evaluations of Educational Interventions
entries.desc: Statistical procedures that correct for multiple testing typically result in hypothesis tests with reduced statistical power because adjustment methods reduce the likelihood of identifying real differences between contrasted groups.

entries.title: Achievement and Attainment in Chicago Charter Schools
entries.desc: During the past decade, charter schools have been one of the fastest growing segments of the K–12 education sector. This is the first report to examine how charter schools may affect long-term attainment, including high-school graduation and college entry.

entries.title: Measuring Teacher Effectiveness in Memphis
entries.desc: Mathematica Policy Research has adapted its school-level value-added model to teacher-level data to help New Leaders for New Schools identify high-performing teachers in award-winning schools within Memphis City Schools.

entries.title: Design for the Evaluation of Early Elementary School Mathematics Curricula
entries.desc: Many U.S. students enter elementary school with poor math skills. Students from different socioeconomic backgrounds also differ in math performance, with students from poor families lagging behind those from more affluent families.

entries.title: Elementary-Grade Math Programs in the Pittsburgh Public Schools: A Comparison of Everyday Mathematics and Harcourt Math
entries.desc: A quasi-experimental analysis of data from the Pittsburgh Public Schools finds no difference in the effects of two elementary-level math programs—Everyday Mathematics and Harcourt Math—on student achievement.

entries.title: When Elementary Schools Stay Open Late: Results from The National Evaluation of the 21st-Century Community Learning Centers Program (Journal Article)
entries.desc: This article presents evidence from the elementary school component of Mathematica’s national evaluation of the 21st Century Community Learning Center afterschool programs.

entries.title: National Assessment of Title I Final Report Volume II: Closing the Reading Gap: Findings from a Randomized Trial of Four Reading Interventions for Striving Readers
entries.desc: This report details findings from an experimental evaluation of four widely used programs for elementary school students with reading problems—Corrective Reading, Failure Free Reading, Spell Read P.A.T., and Wilson Reading.

entries.title: Technical Report of the NAEP Mathematics Assessment in Puerto Rico: Focus on Statistical Issues
entries.desc: In 2003, a trial National Assessment of Educational Progress mathematics assessment was administered in Spanish to public school students in grades 4 and 8 in Puerto Rico.

entries.title: Evaluation of the National Survey of Recent College Graduates (NSRCG) Postsecondary Institution Sample
entries.desc: The National Survey of Recent College Graduates (NSRCG) is a repeated survey conducted every two or three years.

entries.title: Ingredients of a Successful Summer Learning Program: A Case Study of the Building Educated Leaders for Life (BELL) Accelerated Learning Summer Program
entries.desc: This process report was developed as part of a larger study that included a random assignment impact evaluation of this summer program.

entries.title: National Evaluation of Early Reading First
entries.desc: The Early Reading First program provided grants that were designed to enhance teacher practices, instructional content, and classroom environments in preschools to ensure that young children, especially those from low-income families, start school with the skills needed for academic success.

entries.title: Upward Bound Math-Science Program: Description and Interim Impact Estimates
entries.desc: In 1990, the U.S. Department of Education established a math and science initiative within Upward Bound, a federal grant program designed to provide disadvantaged high school students with skills and experiences that will prepare them for college success.

entries.title: The Nation's Report Card: Mathematics 2003 and 2005 Performance in Puerto Rico
entries.desc: This report for the National Center for Education Statistics presents results for the 2003 and 2005 National Assessment of Educational Progress (NAEP) mathematics assessment for fourth- and eighth-grade students in Puerto Rico.

entries.title: Effectiveness of Reading and Mathematics Software Products: Findings from the First Student Cohort
entries.desc: The No Child Left Behind Act called for the U.S. Department of Education to conduct a national study of the effectiveness of educational technology.

entries.title: Issues in Calculating Average Effect Sizes in Meta-Analyses
entries.desc: Presentation at the Application of Effect Sizes in Research on Children and Families: Understanding Impacts on Academic, Emotional Behavioral and Economic Outcomes meeting.

entries.title: Selection Bias in College Admissions Test Scores
entries.desc: Data from the two leading college admissions tests—the SAT and the ACT—can provide a valuable measure of student achievement, but bias resulting from the nonrepresentativeness of test takers is an important concern.

entries.title: Seeding Entrepreneurship Across Campus: Early Implementation Experiences of the Kauffman Campuses Initiative
entries.desc: Entrepreneurship has long been a fundamental aspect of American society, serving as an important contributor to economic growth. However, only recently has entrepreneurship begun to develop as an academic field in U.S. colleges and universities.

entries.title: Gender Gaps in College and High School Graduation by Race, Combining Public and Private Schools
entries.desc: Each year, the U.S. college system grants twice as many bachelor’s degrees to African American females as males. Large gender gaps favoring women also exist for other groups—on average, our college system awards 35 percent more B.A.s to females than males across all racial and ethnic groups.

entries.title: Expanding Beyond Academics: Who Benefits and How?
entries.desc: The growing use of math and reading scores to measure school and student performance, spurred by the No Child Left Behind Act, has heightened an old debate about which competencies public schools should encourage students to develop.

entries.title: Valuing Student Competencies: Which Ones Predict Postsecondary Educational Attainment and Earnings, and for Whom?
entries.desc: This report examines how indicators of academic and nonacademic competencies are related to postsecondary educational and labor market outcomes.

entries.title: Impacts of a Summer Learning Program: A Random Assignment Study of Building Educated Leaders for Life BELL
entries.desc: A growing body of evidence suggests that low-income children's test scores drop significantly relative to their higher-income counterparts during the summer months.

entries.title: A Study of the Effect of Talent Search on Secondary and Postsecondary Outcomes in Florida, Indiana, and Texas
entries.desc: Low-income students and students whose parents have not attended college typically are less likely than middle- and upper-income students to complete high school and attend college, and are thus less likely to reap the benefits of attending college.

entries.title: The Early Reading and Mathematics Achievement of Children Who Repeated Kindergarten or Who Began School a Year Late
entries.desc: This report examines the association between kindergarten enrollment status (repeating kindergarten or delaying entry) and children's first-grade reading and math achievement.

entries.title: The National Evaluation of Reading Comprehension Interventions: Design Report
entries.desc: This report lays out the design for the Evaluation of Reading Comprehension Interventions, which is assessing supplemental reading comprehension programs for fifth-grade students.

entries.title: Closing the Reading Gap: First Year Findings from a Randomized Trial of Four Reading Interventions for Striving Readers
entries.desc: This report presents interim findings from the Power4Kids evaluation.

entries.title: Alternative Routes to Teaching: The Impacts of Teach For America on Student Achievement and Other Outcomes
entries.desc: Based on a randomized experiment to study the impact of Teach For America on student achievement and other outcomes, the authors find that TFA teachers had a positive impact on math achievement and no impact on reading achievement.

entries.title: The NIH Intramural Research Loan Repayment Program: Career Outcomes of Participants and Nonparticipants
entries.desc: For over a decade, the National Institutes of Health has been using repayment of education loans as an incentive to attract and retain first-rate biomedical scientists in its labs and clinics.

entries.title: The Evaluation of Teacher Preparation Models: Design Report
entries.desc: Every year, thousands of new teachers pass through hundreds of different teacher preparation programs and are hired to teach in the nation’s schools. In recent years, alternative programs have expanded rapidly.

entries.title: Integrated Postsecondary Education Data System Data Quality Study
entries.desc: The authors analyze the quality of eight data components from the Integrated Postsecondary Education Data System (IPEDS).

entries.title: Process Analysis on the Operation of the NIH Undergraduate Scholarship Program
entries.desc: The National Institutes of Health (NIH) spends millions of dollars each year to provide scholarships to an elite group of highly talented undergraduate science students from disadvantaged backgrounds to encourage them to pursue careers in biomedical research.

entries.title: When Schools Stay Open Late: The National Evaluation of the 21st-Century Community Learning Centers Program
entries.desc: Elementary students participating in the 21st Century Community Learning Centers program felt safer than students not enrolled but were more likely to engage in negative behaviors.

entries.title: From Kindergarten Through Third Grade: Children's Beginning School Experiences
entries.desc: Using data from the Early Childhood Longitudinal Study, this report highlights children's gains in reading and math during their first four years in school and describes achievement in reading and math at the end of third grade.

entries.title: Interpreting the Evidence from Recent Federal Evaluations of Dropout-Prevention Programs: The State of Scientific Research
entries.desc: Argues that we do not yet have a menu of program options for helping students at risk of dropping out and suggests a way to identify approaches that program developers can use while efforts to develop a stronger scientific basis for new programs continue.

entries.title: When Schools Stay Open Late: The National Evaluation of the 21st-Century Community Learning Centers Program, New Findings
entries.desc: Mathematica's latest report shows that 21st Century after-school programs, which currently spend $1 billion a year in federal funding, changed where and with whom students spent some of their after-school time.

entries.title: Developing the IT Workforce: Certification Programs, Participants, and Outcomes in High Schools and Two-Year Colleges
entries.desc: Skill standards and certification tests have figured prominently in recent efforts to improve schools' information technology classes. Mathematica completed the first national study of high school and college information technology skill certification programs.

entries.title: Factors That Influence Participation in Secondary Vocational Education
entries.desc: Since the early 1990s, federal legislation has encouraged secondary vocational education to go beyond its traditional focus of developing narrowly defined workplace skills to a broader goal of providing career preparation.

entries.title: Quality in the Classroom: How Does Teach For America Measure Up?
entries.desc: This four-page issue brief summarizes findings from Mathematica's evaluation of Teach For America. The researchers found that TFA teachers outperformed non-TFA teachers in the same schools in math and achieved the same results in reading.

entries.title: The Effects of Teach For America on Students: Findings from a National Evaluation (Working Paper)
entries.desc: Students of Teach For America teachers outscored their schoolmates on math achievement tests, and matched their average performance in reading, according to the findings from the first national evaluation of the impact of TFA teachers on student learning.

entries.title: Public Use Files from the National Evaluation of Teach For America
entries.desc: These files from Mathematica’s large-scale random assignment study are for public use by the research community.

entries.title: The Effects of Teach For America on Students: Findings from a National Evaluation
entries.desc: Students of Teach For America teachers outscored their schoolmates on math achievement tests, and matched their average performance in reading, according to the findings from the first national evaluation of the impact of TFA teachers on student learning.

entries.title: Assessing the Effectiveness of Education Interventions: Issues and Recommendations for the Title I Evaluation
entries.desc: Recommends that an upcoming study on the effectiveness of education interventions focus on reading comprehension in grades 3 to 5 and allow for inferences about selected groups of students (such as students with limited English proficiency or with very low reading skills).

entries.title: The Impacts of Regular Upward Bound: Results from the Third Follow-Up Data Collection
entries.desc: Upward Bound is one of the largest and longest running federal programs designed to help economically disadvantaged students go to college.

entries.title: Financial Aid and Students' College Decisions: Evidence from the District of Columbia's Tuition Assistance Grant Program
entries.desc: How college price influences student decisions about whether and where to attend college is an important issue. The District of Columbia Tuition Assistance Grant Program allows those who qualify to attend public colleges and universities in other states at considerably lower "in-state" tuitions.

entries.title: A Study of the Impact of Public School Spending on Postsecondary Educational Attainment Using Statewide School District Refinancing in Kansas
entries.desc: This paper uses a policy change involving statewide school district refinancing in Kansas during the early 1990s to identify the relationship between per-pupil expenditure and the probability that a student will progress to higher education.

entries.title: The Effectiveness of Educational Technology: Issues and Recommendations for the National Study
entries.desc: Recommends that an upcoming study of the effectiveness of educational technology in improving academic achievement focus on four goals: (1) defining what is meant by "educational technology," (2) deciding how to measure effectiveness, (3) clarifying what kinds of students will be studied, and (4) pinpointing how academic achievement will be defined and measured.

entries.title: Identifying Alternative Certification Programs for an Impact Evaluation of Teacher Preparation
entries.desc: A heated debate exists over the effectiveness of a teacher preparation approach called “alternative certification.”

entries.title: Preparing for Productive Careers: Students' Participation In and Use of Career-Focused Learning Activities
entries.desc: Helping students formulate career goals and prepare for successful careers continues to be an important educational objective pursued by many high schools.

entries.title: When Stay Open Late: The National Evaluation of the 21st-Century Community Learning Centers Program, First Year Findings
entries.desc: The first-year findings reveal that while the 21st Century Community Learning Centers changed where and with whom students spent some of their after-school time and increased parental involvement, they had limited influence on academic performance, no influence on feelings of safety or on the number of “latchkey” children, and some negative influences on behavior.

entries.title: Random Assignment in Program Evaluation and Intervention Research: Questions and Answers
entries.desc: This pamphlet, prepared for the Institute of Education Sciences, describes the purpose of education program evaluation, defines random assignment and discusses its advantages over other approaches, and looks at other issues related to use of random assignment in scientifically based education research.

entries.title: Invited Commentary: First Publications from the Schools and Staffing Survey, 1999-2000
entries.desc: Notes the importance of these new data and reports for the education field in studying schools, administrators, and teachers, particularly in light of changes in teacher training in the past two decades.

entries.title: Back to School: Federal Student Aid Policy and Adult College Enrollment
entries.desc: Much of the research examining the question of how federal financial aid affects decisions to enroll in college has focused on the behavior of students immediately following graduation, leaving unanswered the question of how changes in the availability of aid affects the behavior of older students.

entries.title: The NIH Undergraduate Scholarship Program: Issues and Options for Recruiting and Outreach
entries.desc: Provides guidance on key issues affecting the operations of this scholarship program designed to encourage undergraduates from disadvantaged backgrounds to pursue careers in biomedical research.

entries.title: Are Experiments the Only Option? A Look at Dropout Prevention Programs
entries.desc: Explores whether propensity score methods produce unbiased estimates of program impacts, by comparing experimental and propensity score impacts of dropout prevention programs.

entries.title: Monitoring School Quality: An Indicators Report
entries.desc: Explores why some schools may be better than others at helping students learn by identifying and critiquing national indicator data on school leadership, goals, professional community, discipline, academic environment, teacher academic skills, teaching assignment, teacher experience, professional development, course content, pedagogy, technology and class size. Notes that teachers make a difference.

entries.title: Making Schools Career-Focused
entries.desc: Examines a group of high schools that have embraced career majors as the organizing device for school improvement efforts.

entries.title: Making Do With Less: Interpreting the Evidence from Recent Federal Evaluations of Dropout-Prevention Programs
entries.desc: Presentation at the Dropouts in America conference, Harvard University, January 2001.

entries.title: Follow-Up Site Visit Report on Preparing Tomorrow's Teachers to Use Technology: First-Year Objectives Activities and Outcomes from a Sample of 1999 Grantees
entries.desc: Describes the results of a second set of site visits to 10 grantees, highlighting common activities and challenges faced during the first year, as well as impacts at the individual and institutional levels.

entries.title: Enhancing the 21st Century Community Learning Centers Evaluation: A Concept Paper
entries.desc: Describes how various components of Mathematica's complex study fit together and explains perspectives and principles related to 1) distinguishing implemented programs from intended ones, 2) ensuring broad coverage to explore competing theories of effective programming, 3) collecting longitudinal information on students and centers, and 4) measuring program impacts with control/comparison group designs. Also elaborates on the evaluation's conceptual framework.

entries.title: Evaluation of the Federal Talent Search Program Phase II and Phase III: Feasibility Report
entries.desc: Assesses the feasibility of estimating the impacts of the program, which helps disadvantaged middle school and high school students prepare for, apply to, get financial aid for, and begin attending college.

entries.title: Making Joint Commitments: Roles of Schools, Employers, and Students in Implementing National Skill Standards
entries.desc: Discusses the experiences of some of the first vocational education programs to adopt national skill standards, finding that curriculum and assessment changes require external support and that concrete program changes and trade association leadership can engage local employers.

entries.title: An Evaluation of the Horizon Scholarship Program in the Edgewood Independent School District, San Antonio, Texas: The First Year
entries.desc: Looks at a privately funded voucher program, finding minimal differences in math scores and modest differences in reading scores between voucher students and their public school counterparts, while also noting that the voucher program seems to be serving the working poor.

entries.title: Estimating the Payoff to Attending a More Selective College: An Application of Selection on Observables and Unobservables
entries.desc: There are many estimates of the effect of college quality on students' subsequent earnings. One difficulty interpreting past estimates, however, is that elite colleges admit students, in part, based on characteristics that are related to their earnings capacity.

entries.title: The Impacts for Upward Bound: Final Report for Phase I of the National Evaluation
entries.desc: Focuses on Upward Bound's effects on students during high school and the first few years of college, finding limited impacts during high school on students as a whole, but larger impacts on some groups, notably those with lower educational expectations going into the program, those with poorer academic performance as high school freshmen, and those who remain in the program for at least two years.

entries.title: Addressing Literacy Needs at Work: Implementation and Impact of Workplace Literacy Programs
entries.desc: This final report on the national evaluation examines program implementation and effects of workplace literacy, finding that it is possible to implement and institutionalize effective programs, but doing so presents challenges for employers, program leaders, and policymakers.

entries.title: How Can We Help? What We Have Learned from Evaluations of Federal Dropout-Prevention Programs
entries.desc: Summarizes the largest federal study ever conducted of dropout prevention programs, finding three promising models: alternative middle and high schools for those at risk of dropping out, programs aimed at helping older students earn a GED, and whole-school restructuring efforts aimed at dropout prevention.

entries.title: Impacts of School Restructuring Initiatives
entries.desc: A Research Report from the School Dropout Demonstration Assistance Program Evaluation.

entries.title: Do We Know Whom to Serve? Issues in Using Risk Factors to Identify Dropouts
entries.desc: A Research Report from the School Dropout Demonstration Assistance Program Evaluation.

entries.title: Impacts of Dropout Prevention Programs
entries.desc: A Research Report from the School Dropout Demonstration Assistance Program Evaluation.

entries.title: The Short-Term Impact of Upward Bound: An Interim Report
entries.desc: This report describes the impact of Upward Bound (UB) on students' education-related outcomes and course-taking during the first year or two of high school, as well as students' persistence in the UB program.

entries.title: A 1990s View of Upward Bound: Programs Offered, Students Served, and Operational Issues
entries.desc: This report describes the impact of Upward Bound on students' education-related outcomes and course-taking during the first year or two of high school, as well as students' persistence in the Upward Bound program.

entries.title: What Do Effective Teachers of Inner-City Children have in Common?
entries.desc: This paper investigates the extent to which variables describing the classroom behavior of teachers and variables describing teachers' backgrounds and training explain differences in the effectiveness of teachers in improving the vocabulary skills of inner-city black children in four elementary grade levels.

entries.title: Charter Schools’ Response to the Pandemic in California, New York and Washington State
entries.desc: Charter schools in California, New York, and Washington state show standing capacity to adapt. New research on charter schools’ reveals a larger story of how education policy at large...

entries.title: NY charter schools: Remote instruction during the COVID crisis (spring 2020)
entries.desc: New York State charter schools adjusted rapidly to remote learning Download Full Report Press Release

entries.title: Charter School Performance in the State of Washington
entries.desc: Washington’s charter schools a plus for English language learners but neutral on average for most students.

entries.title: Charter School Performance in South Carolina 2019
entries.desc: Charter school students on average fall behind peers in math 1. The typical charter school student in South Carolina experienced similar learning gains in reading and weaker growth in...

entries.title: Charter School Performance in New Mexico
entries.desc: Charter schools on average improve to the level of traditional public schools Full Report

entries.title: Charter School Performance in Pennsylvania
entries.desc: Online charters serve students poorly compared to other schools

entries.title: Charter School Impacts in Idaho
entries.desc: Idaho charter school successes particularly notable in rural areas
